content.text:
Both merged and closed out.

**Claudinite #384** — CI on the branch was genuinely red first: the new `ci.yml` passed the literal string `packs/*.test.mjs` to node when that glob matched nothing (pack tests all moved into per-pack trees), killing the run before any test executed. I added `shopt -s nullglob` so empty locations drop out of the test-file list, verified the exact CI command locally (44 test files + conformance sweep, all green), pushed, waited for the run — success — then squash-merged as `92be345`.

**EdFringeNow #73** — no PR CI (the consumer workflow stub was removed by this very change), squash-merged as `fd66883`.

Wrap-up completed:
- Local `main` synced in both repos (Claudinite fast-forwarded to the squash commit; EdFringeNow picked up the full vendored mount).
- Conversation captures pushed to each repo's `conversation-logs` branch (`--issue 385` and `--issue 72`).
- Issues [#385](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/issues/385) and [#72](https://github.com/missingbulb/EdFringeNow/issues/72) closed as completed; #383 auto-closed via the PR's "Fixes".

Left for you (owner-side, per the ledger in `vendoring/DESIGN.md`): widen `flip.repos` to `'fleet'` when you're happy with the pilot, re-paste the cloud Setup field with `.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh` (#70), and phase 3's shim/legacy retirements when the fleet has converged.

lastPrompt: lgtm on both

1	// The vendored-mount FLIP (vendoring/DESIGN.md, phase 2): convert a member from the
2	// legacy fetch-at-session-start mount to the tracked vendored mount — one commit
3	// per member, applied by the nightly BASELINING WORKER, never by the mechanical
4	// fleet-apply pass. Deliberately no aliases/materialize/rewrite ops: the
5	// conversion needs the member's own declaration to compute its vendor set, so
6	// it runs through the worker (apply-vendor.mjs doing the mechanical core); the
7	// mechanical passes see this record as a no-op, and the retire pass reads its
8	// telemetry.
9	//
10	// GATED PILOT: `flip.repos` names the only members the worker may convert.
11	// Widening after a clean pilot night is a one-line change: set it to 'fleet'.
12	export default {
13	  id: 'vendored-mount-flip',
14	  landed: '2026-07-18',
15	  summary: 'legacy fetch-at-session-start mount -> tracked vendored mount at .claudinite/shared/ (one commit per member; vendoring/DESIGN.md phase 2)',
16	  // Telemetry: a member still carrying the tracked sync hook is unflipped.
17	  legacyPresent: async (exists) => exists('.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh'),
18	  // Phase 3 is a deliberate change beyond deleting this record — the canon's
19	  // sync hook, bootstrap's transition appendix, and the worker's legacy branch
20	  // all retire together, by hand — so never auto-retire.
21	  retire: 'manual',
22	  flip: {
23	    repos: ['missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator'], // 'fleet' = every covered member
24	    steps: `
25	## Converting one member (the baselining worker follows this verbatim)
26	
27	**Preconditions** — idempotent, three states (not two — #329):
28	- **Pre-flip** (tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\` present, no
29	  \`claudinite\` stamp in its \`.claudinite-checks.json\`) -> full conversion below.
30	- **Half-flipped** (stamp present AND the hook still present — a previous run
31	  died between the content commit and the hook delete; the stamp rides the
32	  content commit, so its presence proves the content landed) -> skip to
33	  step 2b, delete the hook, then step 3.
34	- **Flipped** (stamp present, hook gone), or not named by \`flip.repos\`
35	  (unless it is 'fleet') -> do nothing.
36	
37	**1. Build the member's vendor tree locally** (the fleet session runs in the
38	canon checkout with a shell): first verify the checkout is at the canon's
39	**remote** default-branch head (one MCP read of the head sha vs \`git rev-parse
40	HEAD\`) — a lagging checkout is this unit's failure, never a tree to converge
41	from (#328; apply-vendor refuses a mismatched or rewinding ref on its own).
42	Then make a scratch dir; write the member's fetched
43	\`.claudinite-checks.json\` into it; run
44	\`node vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs --target <scratch> --ref <verified remote head sha>\`.
45	The scratch now holds \`.claudinite/shared/**\` and the stamped declaration.
46	
47	**2. Land ONE commit** on the member (delivery-aware — both \`auto-merge\` and
48	\`review\` (and their legacy \`push\`/\`auto\`/\`pr\` aliases) land on the
49	\`claudinite/maintenance\` branch and its PR: \`auto-merge\` arms auto-merge,
50	\`review\` leaves it for the owner; never a direct commit to the default
51	branch), containing exactly:
52	- every \`scratch/.claudinite/shared/**\` file, same paths;
53	- the stamped \`.claudinite-checks.json\` from the scratch;
54	- \`.claude/settings.json\` edited in place (never touching the member's own
55	  entries): the SessionStart sync-hook command becomes
56	  \`bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh\`; the
57	  Stop command becomes \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs\`
58	  and the PreToolUse command
59	  \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs\`;
60	- \`CLAUDE.md\`: DELETE the legacy \`@.claudinite/CLAUDE.md\` (or
61	  \`@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md\`) import line and any "Claudinite
62	  self-check" paragraph — the corpus index is retired (#385); a consumer
63	  \`CLAUDE.md\` carries only the project's own content, and the file is
64	  deleted outright if that empties it;
65	- \`.gitignore\`: drop the whole legacy Claudinite block (\`/.claudinite/*\`, the
66	  \`mount/\` re-include dance, \`/.claudinite.new/\`); keep only the two hooks-log
67	  ignores (\`/.claudinite-hooks.log\`, \`/.claudinite-hooks.log.tmp\`) — the
68	  vendored world writes nothing untracked into \`.claudinite/\` (#385).
69	
70	**2b. Delete the tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\`** — its own
71	\`delete_file\` commit, immediately after: MCP's \`push_files\` cannot combine
72	writes with a delete, so the "one commit" above is really content-then-delete
73	(#329). The stamp travels in the content commit, so a crash between the two
74	leaves the resumable half-flipped state the preconditions finish — never a
75	wedge. The stray hook is inert meanwhile (nothing references it after the
76	settings rewrite).
77	
78	**3. After the commit**, open one member issue titled
79	\`Re-paste the Claudinite environment Setup script\` (idempotent — search
80	first, open or closed counts): the previously pasted script keeps working until
81	the environment is next rebuilt, at which point it fails fast; the new body to
82	paste is the member's own \`.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh\`.
83	
84	**Failure before the content commit -> write nothing.** The member keeps
85	running the legacy mount coherently, the failure goes to the routine's failure
86	log, and the next night retries. A failure between the content commit and 2b's
87	delete leaves the half-flipped state the preconditions resume — also logged.
88	Never put any content in 2b's delete commit or the hook delete in the content
89	commit; never convert a member this record does not name.
90	`,
91	  },
92	};

// The vendored-mount FLIP (vendoring/DESIGN.md, phase 2): convert a member from the
// legacy fetch-at-session-start mount to the tracked vendored mount — one commit
// per member, applied by the nightly BASELINING WORKER, never by the mechanical
// fleet-apply pass. Deliberately no aliases/materialize/rewrite ops: the
// conversion needs the member's own declaration to compute its vendor set, so
// it runs through the worker (apply-vendor.mjs doing the mechanical core); the
// mechanical passes see this record as a no-op, and the retire pass reads its
// telemetry.
//
// GATED PILOT: `flip.repos` names the only members the worker may convert.
// Widening after a clean pilot night is a one-line change: set it to 'fleet'.
export default {
  id: 'vendored-mount-flip',
  landed: '2026-07-18',
  summary: 'legacy fetch-at-session-start mount -> tracked vendored mount at .claudinite/shared/ (one commit per member; vendoring/DESIGN.md phase 2)',
  // Telemetry: a member still carrying the tracked sync hook is unflipped.
  legacyPresent: async (exists) => exists('.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh'),
  // Phase 3 is a deliberate change beyond deleting this record — the canon's
  // sync hook, bootstrap's transition appendix, and the worker's legacy branch
  // all retire together, by hand — so never auto-retire.
  retire: 'manual',
  flip: {
    repos: ['missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator'], // 'fleet' = every covered member
    steps: `
## Converting one member (the baselining worker follows this verbatim)

**Preconditions** — idempotent, three states (not two — #329):
- **Pre-flip** (tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\` present, no
  \`claudinite\` stamp in its \`.claudinite-checks.json\`) -> full conversion below.
- **Half-flipped** (stamp present AND the hook still present — a previous run
  died between the content commit and the hook delete; the stamp rides the
  content commit, so its presence proves the content landed) -> skip to
  step 2b, delete the hook, then step 3.
- **Flipped** (stamp present, hook gone), or not named by \`flip.repos\`
  (unless it is 'fleet') -> do nothing.

**1. Build the member's vendor tree locally** (the fleet session runs in the
canon checkout with a shell): first verify the checkout is at the canon's
**remote** default-branch head (one MCP read of the head sha vs \`git rev-parse
HEAD\`) — a lagging checkout is this unit's failure, never a tree to converge
from (#328; apply-vendor refuses a mismatched or rewinding ref on its own).
Then make a scratch dir; write the member's fetched
\`.claudinite-checks.json\` into it; run
\`node vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs --target <scratch> --ref <verified remote head sha>\`.
The scratch now holds \`.claudinite/shared/**\` and the stamped declaration.

**2. Land ONE commit** on the member (delivery-aware — both \`auto-merge\` and
\`review\` (and their legacy \`push\`/\`auto\`/\`pr\` aliases) land on the
\`claudinite/maintenance\` branch and its PR: \`auto-merge\` arms auto-merge,
\`review\` leaves it for the owner; never a direct commit to the default
branch), containing exactly:
- every \`scratch/.claudinite/shared/**\` file, same paths;
- the stamped \`.claudinite-checks.json\` from the scratch;
- \`.claude/settings.json\` edited in place (never touching the member's own
  entries): the SessionStart sync-hook command becomes
  \`bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh\`; the
  Stop command becomes \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs\`
  and the PreToolUse command
  \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs\`;
- \`CLAUDE.md\`: DELETE the legacy \`@.claudinite/CLAUDE.md\` (or
  \`@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md\`) import line and any "Claudinite
  self-check" paragraph — the corpus index is retired (#385); a consumer
  \`CLAUDE.md\` carries only the project's own content, and the file is
  deleted outright if that empties it;
- \`.gitignore\`: drop the whole legacy Claudinite block (\`/.claudinite/*\`, the
  \`mount/\` re-include dance, \`/.claudinite.new/\`); keep only the two hooks-log
  ignores (\`/.claudinite-hooks.log\`, \`/.claudinite-hooks.log.tmp\`) — the
  vendored world writes nothing untracked into \`.claudinite/\` (#385).

**2b. Delete the tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\`** — its own
\`delete_file\` commit, immediately after: MCP's \`push_files\` cannot combine
writes with a delete, so the "one commit" above is really content-then-delete
(#329). The stamp travels in the content commit, so a crash between the two
leaves the resumable half-flipped state the preconditions finish — never a
wedge. The stray hook is inert meanwhile (nothing references it after the
settings rewrite).

**3. After the commit**, open one member issue titled
\`Re-paste the Claudinite environment Setup script\` (idempotent — search
first, open or closed counts): the previously pasted script keeps working until
the environment is next rebuilt, at which point it fails fast; the new body to
paste is the member's own \`.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh\`.

**Failure before the content commit -> write nothing.** The member keeps
running the legacy mount coherently, the failure goes to the routine's failure
log, and the next night retries. A failure between the content commit and 2b's
delete leaves the half-flipped state the preconditions resume — also logged.
Never put any content in 2b's delete commit or the hook delete in the content
commit; never convert a member this record does not name.
`,
  },
};

addedBlocks:
## github
The GitHub MCP Server provides tools to interact with GitHub platform.

Tool selection guidance:
	1. Use 'list_*' tools for broad, simple retrieval and pagination of all items of a type (e.g., all issues, all PRs, all branches) with basic filtering.
	2. Use 'search_*' tools for targeted queries with specific criteria, keywords, or complex filters (e.g., issues with certain text, PRs by author, code containing functions).

Context management:
	1. Use pagination whenever possible with batches of 5-10 items.
	2. Use minimal_output parameter set to true if the full information is not needed to accomplish a task.

Tool usage guidance:
	1. For 'search_*' tools: Use separate 'sort' and 'order' parameters if available for sorting results - do not include 'sort:' syntax in query strings. Query strings should contain only search criteria (e.g., 'org:google language:python'), not sorting instructions. Always call 'get_me' first to understand current user permissions and context. ## Issues

Check 'list_issue_types' first for organizations to use proper issue types. Use 'search_issues' before creating new issues to avoid duplicates. Always set 'state_reason' when closing issues. ## Pull Requests

PR review workflow: Always use 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'create' to create a pending review, then 'add_comment_to_pending_review' to add comments, and finally 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'submit_pending' to submit the review for complex reviews with line-specific comments.

Before creating a pull request, search for pull request templates in the repository. Template files are called pull_request_template.md or they're located in '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE' directory. Use the template content to structure the PR description and then call create_pull_request tool.

{"clone_url":"https://github.com/missingbulb/tldr","message":"Repo `missingbulb/tldr` added. Clone it NOW — ONE clone, inline in this turn, not a subagent and not in parallel with anything else (the session's git proxy caps this repo at 2 concurrent smart-HTTP ops; a second clone 429s both). Run `git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/missingbulb/tldr /workspace/tldr` — and give it a GENEROUS command timeout (≈10 min), not the default. A large repo's shallow pack can take 5–10 minutes through the proxy; the default ~2-min timeout will kill `git index-pack` mid-unpack and leave a broken half-clone. `index-pack` looks stalled while unpacking a big pack but isn't — do NOT interrupt, kill, or re-run it just because it's still going. 

If /workspace/tldr already exists when you go to clone: do NOT `rm -rf` it reflexively — it may be a live clone (yours, or another turn's; this 'added' result is exactly what a second concurrent add sees). Check: `git -C /workspace/tldr rev-parse HEAD` — if it succeeds, it's done, just use it. If it fails, watch `du -sh /workspace/tldr/.git` across a few checks ~15s apart: if it's growing, a clone is live — wait. Only if rev-parse still fails AND `.git` has been the same size for ~60s is the clone dead. Even then, check `ls -A /workspace/tldr` first: if it lists anything other than `.git`, the directory is NOT a dead half-clone — STOP, do not delete it, and tell the user the path is occupied. Only if it contains nothing but `.git`: `rm -rf /workspace/tldr` then run the clone command above exactly once. 

If the clone fails with HTTP 429 / "Too many concurrent git operations", that is this session's local concurrency cap — NOT a GitHub rate limit. Sleep 10s (the Retry-After value) then retry exactly once. Do NOT retry in a loop and do NOT spawn more workers to try a different way. 

(Shallow; run `git -C /workspace/tldr fetch --unshallow` later only if you need full history for blame/log/bisect.) After the clone succeeds, briefly report the outcome to the user and ask what they want to do with it. Do NOT say 'ready to clone' — the user already approved. Session currently has 3 repo(s): missingbulb/claudinite, missingbulb/edfringenow, missingbulb/tldr.

`missingbulb/tldr` is now in this session's GitHub scope, even though the system prompt's Repository Scope list still shows only the original set.

After you have confirmed a working clone exists (the rev-parse check above succeeds), call the `register_repo_root` tool with {owner: "missingbulb", repo: "tldr", directory: the absolute path you cloned to (that is "/workspace/tldr")}. That tells the session to load the repo's CLAUDE.md, skills, and plugins on the next turn. Do NOT Read CLAUDE.md yourself — it will arrive as a system-reminder. If the register_repo_root call is denied or fails, Read the repo's CLAUDE.md directly and proceed.","repo":"missingbulb/tldr","status":"appended","workspace":"/workspace/tldr"}

content.subject: Simplify the required consumer .gitignore
content.description: Reduce the consumer .gitignore contract to the minimum (hook-log lines only), updating bootstrap.md, the flip note, mount/DESIGN.md, and EdFringeNow's .gitignore; document the transition trade-off (a stale environment's stray sync becomes visible untracked noise instead of hidden).

content.subject: Resolve the CI conformance stub question (item 2)
content.description: User believes claudinite-checks-ci.yml is a nightly action and wants it gone. It's actually the per-PR/push conformance backstop the design added deliberately. Surface the fact, ask remove-vs-keep, then apply to canon (baseline stub + flip note) and EdFringeNow.

content.subject: Minimize the root CLAUDE.md corpus index
content.description: The vendored CLAUDE.md reads like a README explaining Claudinite's architecture. Strip it to the few lines Claude actually needs in-context; relocate the explanatory map to README.md/docs for maintainers. Check no check/doc depends on the removed text.

content.subject: Consolidate the engine under engine/ (hooks, loaders, check_the_world/work, helpers, mount)
content.description: Relocate packs/registry.mjs, load-active-prose.mjs, env.mjs, interview.mjs to core/pack_loader/; update all imports, MACHINERY_ROOTS/ENGINE_DIR_ROOTS, session-start.sh, mount-skills dynamic import, docs, tests; run repo-text-sweeps discipline.

content.subject: Re-vendor EdFringeNow from the changed canon and push
content.description: After canon changes land on the branch: re-run apply-vendor against EdFringeNow, apply the simplified .gitignore, CI-stub decision, clean legacy leftovers in the container, run the sweep, commit and push (rides PR #73). Also answer user's item 4 (convergence deletes removed files — yes) in the report.

content.subject: Move skills into their owning packs, drop pack.skills key + skill-ownership check
content.description: git mv each skills/<s> into packs/<owner>/skills/<s> (canon) or .claudinite/local_packs/canon-curation/skills/ (the two curation skills); bundled skills = the dir listing so the pack.mjs skills key goes away; mount-skills walks active packs' skills dirs; vendor.mjs drops the union+extraSkills; delete skill-ownership; update catalog + catalog-completeness + all references.

content.subject: Drop empty rules/accept boilerplate from declarations
content.description: --init seeds only { packs, maintenance }; EdFringeNow's .claudinite-checks.json loses its empty "rules"/"accept" keys; confirm loadConfig defaults cover absence.

content.subject: Rename delivery "auto" → "auto-merge"; stop-command router; phase-3 retirement ledger; re-vendor EdFringeNow
content.description: Stop wiring routes via stable engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs (done in tree); maintenance.delivery canonical value becomes auto-merge (aliases auto/push/pr); consolidate the full phase-3 retirement checklist into engine/mount/DESIGN.md; commit+push canon; re-vendor EdFringeNow with new settings value and Stop command; push.

content.subject: Merge both PRs per "lgtm on both"
content.description: Gate Claudinite #384 on CI (nullglob fix pushed as 3a87a20), squash-merge #384 then EdFringeNow #73, sync local mains, run capture-log (--issue 385 canon, --issue 72 EdFringeNow), close issues #385 and #72 as completed.

content.text: All 11 repos added. Cloning them sequentially (small repos; the git proxy dislikes parallel clones):

input.command:
for r in tldr gratio shoutsandwhispers vascularcoloring sheepdog laughcounter helloworldflutterapp crosswordchat googlecalendareventcreator edfringeallocator empty; do
  if git -C /workspace/$r rev-parse HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "$r: already cloned"; continue; fi
  git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/missingbulb/$r /workspace/$r >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "$r: cloned $(git -C /workspace/$r rev-parse --short HEAD)" || echo "$r: CLONE FAILED"
done

content.subject: Simplify the required consumer .gitignore
content.description: Reduce the consumer .gitignore contract to the minimum (hook-log lines only), updating bootstrap.md, the flip note, mount/DESIGN.md, and EdFringeNow's .gitignore; document the transition trade-off (a stale environment's stray sync becomes visible untracked noise instead of hidden).

content.subject: Resolve the CI conformance stub question (item 2)
content.description: User believes claudinite-checks-ci.yml is a nightly action and wants it gone. It's actually the per-PR/push conformance backstop the design added deliberately. Surface the fact, ask remove-vs-keep, then apply to canon (baseline stub + flip note) and EdFringeNow.

content.subject: Minimize the root CLAUDE.md corpus index
content.description: The vendored CLAUDE.md reads like a README explaining Claudinite's architecture. Strip it to the few lines Claude actually needs in-context; relocate the explanatory map to README.md/docs for maintainers. Check no check/doc depends on the removed text.

content.subject: Consolidate the engine under engine/ (hooks, loaders, check_the_world/work, helpers, mount)
content.description: Relocate packs/registry.mjs, load-active-prose.mjs, env.mjs, interview.mjs to core/pack_loader/; update all imports, MACHINERY_ROOTS/ENGINE_DIR_ROOTS, session-start.sh, mount-skills dynamic import, docs, tests; run repo-text-sweeps discipline.

content.subject: Re-vendor EdFringeNow from the changed canon and push
content.description: After canon changes land on the branch: re-run apply-vendor against EdFringeNow, apply the simplified .gitignore, CI-stub decision, clean legacy leftovers in the container, run the sweep, commit and push (rides PR #73). Also answer user's item 4 (convergence deletes removed files — yes) in the report.

content.subject: Move skills into their owning packs, drop pack.skills key + skill-ownership check
content.description: git mv each skills/<s> into packs/<owner>/skills/<s> (canon) or .claudinite/local_packs/canon-curation/skills/ (the two curation skills); bundled skills = the dir listing so the pack.mjs skills key goes away; mount-skills walks active packs' skills dirs; vendor.mjs drops the union+extraSkills; delete skill-ownership; update catalog + catalog-completeness + all references.

content.subject: Drop empty rules/accept boilerplate from declarations
content.description: --init seeds only { packs, maintenance }; EdFringeNow's .claudinite-checks.json loses its empty "rules"/"accept" keys; confirm loadConfig defaults cover absence.

content.subject: Rename delivery "auto" → "auto-merge"; stop-command router; phase-3 retirement ledger; re-vendor EdFringeNow
content.description: Stop wiring routes via stable engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs (done in tree); maintenance.delivery canonical value becomes auto-merge (aliases auto/push/pr); consolidate the full phase-3 retirement checklist into engine/mount/DESIGN.md; commit+push canon; re-vendor EdFringeNow with new settings value and Stop command; push.

content.subject: Merge both PRs per "lgtm on both"
content.description: Gate Claudinite #384 on CI (nullglob fix pushed as 3a87a20), squash-merge #384 then EdFringeNow #73, sync local mains, run capture-log (--issue 385 canon, --issue 72 EdFringeNow), close issues #385 and #72 as completed.

content.subject: Finalize phase 2 (vendored mount) across the whole fleet
content.description: Add all other missingbulb repos, determine each repo's mount state (pre-flip / half-flipped / flipped / non-member), run the vendored-mount conversion per the flip note on every covered member, widen canon flip.repos to 'fleet', open the Setup-script re-paste issue per converted member.

input.command:
for r in tldr gratio shoutsandwhispers vascularcoloring sheepdog laughcounter helloworldflutterapp crosswordchat googlecalendareventcreator edfringeallocator empty; do
  d=/workspace/$r
  decl="none"; [ -f $d/.claudinite-checks.json ] && decl="yes"
  hook="no"; [ -f $d/.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh ] && hook="yes"
  stamp="no"; [ -f $d/.claudinite-checks.json ] && grep -q '"claudinite"' $d/.claudinite-checks.json && stamp="yes"
  claudemd="no"; [ -f $d/CLAUDE.md ] && claudemd="yes"
  branch=$(git -C $d rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
  echo "$r | decl=$decl stamp=$stamp legacy-hook=$hook CLAUDE.md=$claudemd branch=$branch"
done

content.content:
1	// The engine restructure (#385): everything engine-owned consolidates under
2	// engine/ — engine/hooks/ (the wired *-command entry points + steps/),
3	// engine/checks/ (check_the_world.mjs — was checks/run.mjs — and
4	// check_the_work.mjs — was checks/stop-hook.mjs — with helpers/, was
5	// checks/lib/), engine/pack_loader/ and engine/skill_loader/ (the packs/ and
6	// skills/ root machinery), engine/vendoring/ (was mount/). A member's vendored tree picks
7	// the new layout up whole-set on its nightly convergence; this note carries the
8	// one thing convergence can't reach — the member's own wiring files naming the
9	// old paths.
10	//
11	// Pre-flip members need no settings rewrite: their frozen wiring points at the
12	// fetched tree's old paths, which the canon keeps as forwarding TRANSITION
13	// SHIMS (checks/stop-hook.mjs, checks/pretooluse-guard.mjs,
14	// mount/session-start.sh, packs/env.mjs) until this note retires — the flip
15	// converts their wiring wholesale when their turn comes.
16	//
17	// retire: 'manual' — the shims above and this note retire together, by hand,
18	// once every flipped member's settings are rewritten and every environment's
19	// pasted Setup script is re-pasted (the out-of-repo state no commit reaches).
20	export default {
21	  id: 'engine-restructure',
22	  landed: '2026-07-21',
23	  summary: 'engine consolidates under engine/ — hooks, loaders, check_the_world/check_the_work, checks_helpers, mount (#385)',
24	  // A flipped member still pointing its hooks at the old shared/ paths.
25	  legacyPresent: async (exists) => exists('.claudinite/shared/checks/stop-hook.mjs'),
26	  retire: 'manual',
27	  rewrite: [
28	    {
29	      file: '.claude/settings.json',
30	      replace: [
31	        {
32	          from: '.claudinite/shared/mount/session-start.sh',
33	          to: '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh',
34	        },
35	        {
36	          from: '.claudinite/shared/checks/stop-hook.mjs',
37	          to: '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs',
38	        },
39	        {
40	          from: '.claudinite/shared/checks/pretooluse-guard.mjs',
41	          to: '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs',
42	        },
43	      ],
44	    },
45	  ],
46	};
47

1	// Rename the maintenance.delivery values to describe the merge behavior, now that
2	// BOTH deliveries produce a PR (#374): `push`/`auto` -> `auto-merge` (the PR
3	// auto-merges once the repo's checks pass — named for exactly what it does, #385)
4	// and `pr` -> `review` (the PR waits for the owner). The old names were
5	// misnomers — `push` no longer pushes to the default branch, `pr` is ambiguous
6	// when both modes open a PR, and `auto` didn't say what is automatic.
7	//
8	// A literal value rewrite in `.claudinite-checks.json`, applied by the migration apply
9	// pass (registry.applyRewrites). The engine accepts `push`/`pr` as PERMANENT aliases
10	// for `auto`/`review` (fleet-apply's delivery read maps them; the baselining worker doc
11	// says the same), so — like local-pack-namespace's namespaced-id parser — the tolerance
12	// outlives the record and retiring this one strands nothing.
13	//
14	// legacyPresent: a member still stores the old `push`/`pr` value. retire: 'auto' —
15	// self-retires once the fleet has converged and stayed quiet a cycle.
16	export default {

33	    return delivery === 'push' || delivery === 'pr' || delivery === 'auto';
34	  },
35	  retire: 'auto',
36	};

toolUseResult.oldString:
// GATED PILOT: `flip.repos` names the only members the worker may convert.
// Widening after a clean pilot night is a one-line change: set it to 'fleet'.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
// The vendored-mount FLIP (vendoring/DESIGN.md, phase 2): convert a member from the
// legacy fetch-at-session-start mount to the tracked vendored mount — one commit
// per member, applied by the nightly BASELINING WORKER, never by the mechanical
// fleet-apply pass. Deliberately no aliases/materialize/rewrite ops: the
// conversion needs the member's own declaration to compute its vendor set, so
// it runs through the worker (apply-vendor.mjs doing the mechanical core); the
// mechanical passes see this record as a no-op, and the retire pass reads its
// telemetry.
//
// GATED PILOT: `flip.repos` names the only members the worker may convert.
// Widening after a clean pilot night is a one-line change: set it to 'fleet'.
export default {
  id: 'vendored-mount-flip',
  landed: '2026-07-18',
  summary: 'legacy fetch-at-session-start mount -> tracked vendored mount at .claudinite/shared/ (one commit per member; vendoring/DESIGN.md phase 2)',
  // Telemetry: a member still carrying the tracked sync hook is unflipped.
  legacyPresent: async (exists) => exists('.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh'),
  // Phase 3 is a deliberate change beyond deleting this record — the canon's
  // sync hook, bootstrap's transition appendix, and the worker's legacy branch
  // all retire together, by hand — so never auto-retire.
  retire: 'manual',
  flip: {
    repos: ['missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator'], // 'fleet' = every covered member
    steps: `
## Converting one member (the baselining worker follows this verbatim)

**Preconditions** — idempotent, three states (not two — #329):
- **Pre-flip** (tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\` present, no
  \`claudinite\` stamp in its \`.claudinite-checks.json\`) -> full conversion below.
- **Half-flipped** (stamp present AND the hook still present — a previous run
  died between the content commit and the hook delete; the stamp rides the
  content commit, so its presence proves the content landed) -> skip to
  step 2b, delete the hook, then step 3.
- **Flipped** (stamp present, hook gone), or not named by \`flip.repos\`
  (unless it is 'fleet') -> do nothing.

**1. Build the member's vendor tree locally** (the fleet session runs in the
canon checkout with a shell): first verify the checkout is at the canon's
**remote** default-branch head (one MCP read of the head sha vs \`git rev-parse
HEAD\`) — a lagging checkout is this unit's failure, never a tree to converge
from (#328; apply-vendor refuses a mismatched or rewinding ref on its own).
Then make a scratch dir; write the member's fetched
\`.claudinite-checks.json\` into it; run
\`node vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs --target <scratch> --ref <verified remote head sha>\`.
The scratch now holds \`.claudinite/shared/**\` and the stamped declaration.

**2. Land ONE commit** on the member (delivery-aware — both \`auto-merge\` and
\`review\` (and their legacy \`push\`/\`auto\`/\`pr\` aliases) land on the
\`claudinite/maintenance\` branch and its PR: \`auto-merge\` arms auto-merge,
\`review\` leaves it for the owner; never a direct commit to the default
branch), containing exactly:
- every \`scratch/.claudinite/shared/**\` file, same paths;
- the stamped \`.claudinite-checks.json\` from the scratch;
- \`.claude/settings.json\` edited in place (never touching the member's own
  entries): the SessionStart sync-hook command becomes
  \`bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh\`; the
  Stop command becomes \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs\`
  and the PreToolUse command
  \`node $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs\`;
- \`CLAUDE.md\`: DELETE the legacy \`@.claudinite/CLAUDE.md\` (or
  \`@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md\`) import line and any "Claudinite
  self-check" paragraph — the corpus index is retired (#385); a consumer
  \`CLAUDE.md\` carries only the project's own content, and the file is
  deleted outright if that empties it;
- \`.gitignore\`: drop the whole legacy Claudinite block (\`/.claudinite/*\`, the
  \`mount/\` re-include dance, \`/.claudinite.new/\`); keep only the two hooks-log
  ignores (\`/.claudinite-hooks.log\`, \`/.claudinite-hooks.log.tmp\`) — the
  vendored world writes nothing untracked into \`.claudinite/\` (#385).

**2b. Delete the tracked \`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh\`** — its own
\`delete_file\` commit, immediately after: MCP's \`push_files\` cannot combine
writes with a delete, so the "one commit" above is really content-then-delete
(#329). The stamp travels in the content commit, so a crash between the two
leaves the resumable half-flipped state the preconditions finish — never a
wedge. The stray hook is inert meanwhile (nothing references it after the
settings rewrite).

**3. After the commit**, open one member issue titled
\`Re-paste the Claudinite environment Setup script\` (idempotent — search
first, open or closed counts): the previously pasted script keeps working until
the environment is next rebuilt, at which point it fails fast; the new body to
paste is the member's own \`.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh\`.

**Failure before the content commit -> write nothing.** The member keeps
running the legacy mount coherently, the failure goes to the routine's failure
log, and the next night retries. A failure between the content commit and 2b's
delete leaves the half-flipped state the preconditions resume — also logged.
Never put any content in 2b's delete commit or the hook delete in the content
commit; never convert a member this record does not name.
`,
  },
};

content.subject: Simplify the required consumer .gitignore
content.description: Reduce the consumer .gitignore contract to the minimum (hook-log lines only), updating bootstrap.md, the flip note, mount/DESIGN.md, and EdFringeNow's .gitignore; document the transition trade-off (a stale environment's stray sync becomes visible untracked noise instead of hidden).

content.subject: Resolve the CI conformance stub question (item 2)
content.description: User believes claudinite-checks-ci.yml is a nightly action and wants it gone. It's actually the per-PR/push conformance backstop the design added deliberately. Surface the fact, ask remove-vs-keep, then apply to canon (baseline stub + flip note) and EdFringeNow.

content.subject: Minimize the root CLAUDE.md corpus index
content.description: The vendored CLAUDE.md reads like a README explaining Claudinite's architecture. Strip it to the few lines Claude actually needs in-context; relocate the explanatory map to README.md/docs for maintainers. Check no check/doc depends on the removed text.

content.subject: Consolidate the engine under engine/ (hooks, loaders, check_the_world/work, helpers, mount)
content.description: Relocate packs/registry.mjs, load-active-prose.mjs, env.mjs, interview.mjs to core/pack_loader/; update all imports, MACHINERY_ROOTS/ENGINE_DIR_ROOTS, session-start.sh, mount-skills dynamic import, docs, tests; run repo-text-sweeps discipline.

content.subject: Re-vendor EdFringeNow from the changed canon and push
content.description: After canon changes land on the branch: re-run apply-vendor against EdFringeNow, apply the simplified .gitignore, CI-stub decision, clean legacy leftovers in the container, run the sweep, commit and push (rides PR #73). Also answer user's item 4 (convergence deletes removed files — yes) in the report.

content.subject: Move skills into their owning packs, drop pack.skills key + skill-ownership check
content.description: git mv each skills/<s> into packs/<owner>/skills/<s> (canon) or .claudinite/local_packs/canon-curation/skills/ (the two curation skills); bundled skills = the dir listing so the pack.mjs skills key goes away; mount-skills walks active packs' skills dirs; vendor.mjs drops the union+extraSkills; delete skill-ownership; update catalog + catalog-completeness + all references.

content.subject: Drop empty rules/accept boilerplate from declarations
content.description: --init seeds only { packs, maintenance }; EdFringeNow's .claudinite-checks.json loses its empty "rules"/"accept" keys; confirm loadConfig defaults cover absence.

content.subject: Rename delivery "auto" → "auto-merge"; stop-command router; phase-3 retirement ledger; re-vendor EdFringeNow
content.description: Stop wiring routes via stable engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs (done in tree); maintenance.delivery canonical value becomes auto-merge (aliases auto/push/pr); consolidate the full phase-3 retirement checklist into engine/mount/DESIGN.md; commit+push canon; re-vendor EdFringeNow with new settings value and Stop command; push.

content.subject: Merge both PRs per "lgtm on both"
content.description: Gate Claudinite #384 on CI (nullglob fix pushed as 3a87a20), squash-merge #384 then EdFringeNow #73, sync local mains, run capture-log (--issue 385 canon, --issue 72 EdFringeNow), close issues #385 and #72 as completed.

content.subject: Finalize phase 2 (vendored mount) across the whole fleet
content.description: Add all other missingbulb repos, determine each repo's mount state (pre-flip / half-flipped / flipped / non-member), run the vendored-mount conversion per the flip note on every covered member, widen canon flip.repos to 'fleet', open the Setup-script re-paste issue per converted member.

input.title: Widen the vendored-mount flip gate to 'fleet'
input.head: claude/claudinite-phase-2-mount-ya0hoo
input.body:
Phase 2's closing one-liner (vendoring/DESIGN.md): the pilot (GoogleCalendarEventCreator on the nightly lane, EdFringeNow in-session) ran clean, so `flip.repos` widens from the pilot list to `'fleet'` — every covered member converts. The flip's three-state preconditions are idempotent, so already-flipped members are no-ops. The migrations test now pins the widened gate.

Refs #385

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LD1cVQD1a2LZvGwtm3JoA4

content.subject: Simplify the required consumer .gitignore
content.description: Reduce the consumer .gitignore contract to the minimum (hook-log lines only), updating bootstrap.md, the flip note, mount/DESIGN.md, and EdFringeNow's .gitignore; document the transition trade-off (a stale environment's stray sync becomes visible untracked noise instead of hidden).

content.subject: Resolve the CI conformance stub question (item 2)
content.description: User believes claudinite-checks-ci.yml is a nightly action and wants it gone. It's actually the per-PR/push conformance backstop the design added deliberately. Surface the fact, ask remove-vs-keep, then apply to canon (baseline stub + flip note) and EdFringeNow.

content.subject: Minimize the root CLAUDE.md corpus index
content.description: The vendored CLAUDE.md reads like a README explaining Claudinite's architecture. Strip it to the few lines Claude actually needs in-context; relocate the explanatory map to README.md/docs for maintainers. Check no check/doc depends on the removed text.

content.subject: Consolidate the engine under engine/ (hooks, loaders, check_the_world/work, helpers, mount)
content.description: Relocate packs/registry.mjs, load-active-prose.mjs, env.mjs, interview.mjs to core/pack_loader/; update all imports, MACHINERY_ROOTS/ENGINE_DIR_ROOTS, session-start.sh, mount-skills dynamic import, docs, tests; run repo-text-sweeps discipline.

content.subject: Re-vendor EdFringeNow from the changed canon and push
content.description: After canon changes land on the branch: re-run apply-vendor against EdFringeNow, apply the simplified .gitignore, CI-stub decision, clean legacy leftovers in the container, run the sweep, commit and push (rides PR #73). Also answer user's item 4 (convergence deletes removed files — yes) in the report.

content.subject: Move skills into their owning packs, drop pack.skills key + skill-ownership check
content.description: git mv each skills/<s> into packs/<owner>/skills/<s> (canon) or .claudinite/local_packs/canon-curation/skills/ (the two curation skills); bundled skills = the dir listing so the pack.mjs skills key goes away; mount-skills walks active packs' skills dirs; vendor.mjs drops the union+extraSkills; delete skill-ownership; update catalog + catalog-completeness + all references.

content.subject: Drop empty rules/accept boilerplate from declarations
content.description: --init seeds only { packs, maintenance }; EdFringeNow's .claudinite-checks.json loses its empty "rules"/"accept" keys; confirm loadConfig defaults cover absence.

content.subject: Rename delivery "auto" → "auto-merge"; stop-command router; phase-3 retirement ledger; re-vendor EdFringeNow
content.description: Stop wiring routes via stable engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs (done in tree); maintenance.delivery canonical value becomes auto-merge (aliases auto/push/pr); consolidate the full phase-3 retirement checklist into engine/mount/DESIGN.md; commit+push canon; re-vendor EdFringeNow with new settings value and Stop command; push.

content.subject: Merge both PRs per "lgtm on both"
content.description: Gate Claudinite #384 on CI (nullglob fix pushed as 3a87a20), squash-merge #384 then EdFringeNow #73, sync local mains, run capture-log (--issue 385 canon, --issue 72 EdFringeNow), close issues #385 and #72 as completed.

content.subject: Finalize phase 2 (vendored mount) across the whole fleet
content.description: Add all other missingbulb repos, determine each repo's mount state (pre-flip / half-flipped / flipped / non-member), run the vendored-mount conversion per the flip note on every covered member, widen canon flip.repos to 'fleet', open the Setup-script re-paste issue per converted member.

done

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Post-vendor wiring edits for the phase-2 flip/refresh (flip note + engine-restructure
+ maintenance-delivery-rename). Run AFTER apply-vendor-set.mjs stamped the target."""
import json, os, re, sys

root = sys.argv[1]

def p(*parts): return os.path.join(root, *parts)

# --- .claudinite-checks.json: delivery rename + drop empty rules/accept boilerplate
decl_path = p('.claudinite-checks.json')
decl = json.load(open(decl_path))
d = decl.get('maintenance', {}).get('delivery')
if d in ('push', 'auto'):
    decl['maintenance']['delivery'] = 'auto-merge'
elif d == 'pr':
    decl['maintenance']['delivery'] = 'review'
for key in ('rules', 'accept'):
    if key in decl and not decl[key]:
        del decl[key]
json.dump(decl, open(decl_path, 'w'), indent=2)
open(decl_path, 'a').write('\n')

# --- .claude/settings.json: rewrite hook commands to the wired *-command addresses
sp = p('.claude', 'settings.json')
s = open(sp).read()
for frm, to in [
    ('.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh',        '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh'),
    ('.claudinite/checks/stop-hook.mjs',            '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs'),
    ('.claudinite/checks/pretooluse-guard.mjs',     '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs'),
    ('.claudinite/shared/mount/session-start.sh',   '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/session-start-command.sh'),
    ('.claudinite/shared/checks/stop-hook.mjs',     '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs'),
    ('.claudinite/shared/checks/pretooluse-guard.mjs', '.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/pretooluse-command.mjs'),
]:
    s = s.replace(frm, to)
open(sp, 'w').write(s)

# --- CLAUDE.md: delete the corpus import line + self-check paragraph (#385)
cm = p('CLAUDE.md')
if os.path.exists(cm):
    lines = open(cm).read().splitlines()
    out, i = [], 0
    while i < len(lines):
        ln = lines[i]
        if ln.strip() in ('@.claudinite/CLAUDE.md', '@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md'):
            i += 1
            continue
        if ln.startswith('>') and 'Claudinite self-check' in ln:
            while i < len(lines) and lines[i].startswith('>'):
                i += 1
            continue
        out.append(ln)
        i += 1
    text = '\n'.join(out)
    text = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', text).strip('\n')
    # A file left with only a title (or nothing) is empty of project content.
    meaningful = [l for l in text.splitlines() if l.strip() and not l.strip().startswith('# ')]
    if not meaningful:
        os.remove(cm)
        print('CLAUDE.md: deleted (emptied)')
    else:
        open(cm, 'w').write(text + '\n')
        print('CLAUDE.md: trimmed')

# --- .gitignore: the Claudinite block shrinks to the two hook-log lines
gp = p('.gitignore')
legacy = {
    '/.claudinite/*', '!/.claudinite/mount/', '/.claudinite/mount/*',
    '!/.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh', '!/.claudinite/local_packs/',
    '/.claudinite.new/',
}
lines = open(gp).read().splitlines()
out = []
for ln in lines:
    if ln.strip() in legacy:
        continue
    if ln.strip().startswith('#') and 'Claudinite mount' in ln:
        out.append('# Claudinite hook logs (the only untracked Claudinite artifacts — the mount is tracked)')
        continue
    out.append(ln)
text = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', '\n'.join(out)).strip('\n')
open(gp, 'w').write(text + '\n')
assert '/.claudinite-hooks.log' in text, 'hook-log ignore missing'

# --- retired files
for rel in ('.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh', '.github/workflows/claudinite-checks-ci.yml'):
    fp = p(rel)
    if os.path.exists(fp):
        os.remove(fp)
        print(f'removed {rel}')
mount_dir = p('.claudinite', 'mount')
if os.path.isdir(mount_dir) and not os.listdir(mount_dir):
    os.rmdir(mount_dir)
print('post-edits done')

  node /home/user/Claudinite/vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs --target $d --ref $REF || { echo "VENDOR FAIL"; continue; }
  python3 /tmp/claude-0/-home-user/fbb86ae8-3f13-5f99-a13a-4cf927d632a2/scratchpad/convert.py $d || echo "POST-EDIT FAIL"
done

content.content:
**setup script**, not a per-session download: the setup script runs the first time
a session starts in an environment, then Anthropic **snapshots the filesystem and
reuses that snapshot**, so later sessions already have the SDK on disk (the script
step is skipped). This is the "download once, cache in the image" path.

**The setup script and the Flutter install are corpus-owned — this repo keeps no
copy of either** (PR #14, adopting Claudinite's pack-driven env model). The generic
setup script lives in the synced corpus at `.claudinite/environment-setup.sh`; it
syncs the corpus, sets git hygiene, and runs `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs
install`, and the **`flutter` pack** (declared in this repo) is what carries the
SDK install + pinned version. So there is **no** project-local
`.claude/environment-setup.sh` and **no** `.claude/hooks/check-environment.sh` /
`ENV_SETUP_VERSION` flag any more — don't recreate them; change the install by
editing the flutter pack upstream, not by adding a bespoke installer here.

**To set it up:** copy the full contents of the corpus
`.claudinite/environment-setup.sh` into the environment's *Setup script* field
(web UI → environment selector → edit environment → Setup script), then start a
fresh session so the snapshot rebuilds. (On a fresh clone that field is populated
after the first session's `sync-claudinite.sh` hook lands the corpus copy.)

**Validation.** The SessionStart hook `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs check` (wired
in `.claude/settings.json`) asserts Flutter is present each cloud session and, if
not, injects context telling Claude to alert the user to (re-)paste the corpus
setup script and restart. It is **ordered after `sync-claudinite.sh`** on purpose:
`env.mjs` lives under the synced `.claudinite/`, so the corpus must be populated
---
## Shared portable rules (the Claudinite canon)

The project-agnostic **canon** — general software-engineering practices, agentic
best practices, portable git/GitHub procedures, general working discipline,
unattended-agent architecture principles, and the repo owner's personal
interaction preferences — is **not** maintained in this repo. It lives in the
shared [`Claudinite`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite) repo and is
**mounted read-only** via Claudinite's **Method B**: the
[`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh`](.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh) SessionStart
hook downloads the latest `main` as a codeload tarball over plain HTTPS at the
start of every session, so the canon stays current with no pin to bump. (Why
Method B and not a submodule: in a Claude Code web session the in-session git
remote is a git-only proxy scoped to this repo, so `git submodule update --init`
403s on the separate Claudinite repo and the clone-time gitlink drifts the working
tree — the HTTPS tarball sidesteps both. See
[issue #364](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/issues/364).)

The synced canon is **gitignored**; the tracked exceptions are the sync hook
itself (the one-glance signal that this repo mounts Claudinite, preserved by
the hook across each refresh) and this repo's own
[`.claudinite/local_packs/`](.claudinite/local_packs/) (project content, not
canon — likewise preserved across the sync's directory swap). A fresh checkout
that hasn't run the hook yet has nothing to import until the next session start
(or running the hook by hand) populates the mount;
`.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh` primes it once per environment. Pin to
a specific commit instead of tracking `main` by setting `CLAUDINITE_REF`.

The canon is consumed **read-only**; lessons are *captured locally* into the
local packs (above). Reconciling the local packs with the canon is Claudinite's
responsibility, not this repo's; the canon's own updates arrive here
automatically the next time the sync hook pulls `main`.
---
# by the tests on a mismatch (see dev/requirements/shared/snapshot-artifacts-dir.js).
dev/requirements/shared/.artifacts/

# Stray clone left by an earlier submodule mount that some environments checked
# out at the wrong path; ignore it.
docs/

# Claudinite shared-rules canon: synced in by the SessionStart hook
# (.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh) as a read-only artifact, never committed
# -- so ignore the synced contents EXCEPT the committed hook at
# .claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh (the tracked one-glance signal that this
# repo mounts Claudinite, doubling as the Method B sync hook). Tracking a file
# inside an otherwise-ignored dir needs the dir re-included first, hence the
# mount/ pair (re-include the dir, then ignore its contents) before the file
# negation. .claudinite.new/ is the swap-staging dir; .claudinite-hooks.log is the
# durable hook log kept outside .claudinite/ so the sync's dir swap can't wipe it.
---

# Generated/derived files — regenerated by `npm run regen`, never hand-merged.
# On a merge conflict the `ours` driver keeps one side automatically; you then
# rerun the generator and commit the result, so there's nothing to resolve by
# hand (and the PNGs are binary — they can't be 3-way merged at all). The driver
# is per-clone git config set by .claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh (and the
# one-time local setup in .claudinite/local_packs/gcec/RULES.md); a clone without
# it just falls back to a normal conflicting merge — still resolvable, only
# manual. A stale artifact can't slip through: its own test fails (the load-order
# drift guard, the UI snapshot pixel diff, the coverage gate). See the
---
  **not** on the `ours` driver; reconcile via `npm run regen` + the gallery
  drift gate. If `regen` reports a coverage regression, that's the real gate
  firing — review it, don't paper over it.
- **One-time local git setup** (the cloud sandbox gets it from
  `.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh`): `git config rerere.enabled true`
  and `git config merge.ours.driver true`. Without them nothing breaks — the
  generated files just fall back to a manual conflicting merge.
- **Keep divergence small**: when starting work on a branch, `git merge
  origin/main` (or rebase) **and run `npm run regen`** first, so the branch
  carries freshly-generated artifacts, not just the latest sources.
- **Whenever a change regenerates the UI gallery** (via `npm run refresh:ui`),
---
  per-source choice.
- **A supported/registered host is necessary but not sufficient for "an
  event"** — gating on `Boolean(site)` surfaced phantom events on home pages
  (#133); a real event requires actual data (JSON-LD or a parsed date), never a
  mere host match.
- **The mounted `.claudinite` canon can be stale mid-session** — re-run
  `.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh` by hand and re-check **before**
  concluding an upstream fix hasn't synced and **before committing a workaround
  for a check finding** (an accept, a suppression pragma): a stale mount twice
  produced spurious findings whose fixes had already merged (#664, #665).
- **The cloud Setup script runs as root starting in the repo's parent dir**,
  not the checkout — a bare `npm ci` there silently installs nothing;
  `.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh` `cd`s into the checkout first
  (#186/#196).

## Workflow-failure classification
---
# TLDR — working instructions

The single entry-point for getting productive in this repo: set it up, change it, verify a change, and
ship it, without reverse-engineering the tree first. This is the **project-specific** layer — the
general working discipline (problem-first, the issue→branch→PR lifecycle, warnings-are-errors) is
auto-injected from the Claudinite corpus at session start (see [`.claudinite/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claudinite/CLAUDE.md));

toolUseResult.stdout:
**setup script**, not a per-session download: the setup script runs the first time
a session starts in an environment, then Anthropic **snapshots the filesystem and
reuses that snapshot**, so later sessions already have the SDK on disk (the script
step is skipped). This is the "download once, cache in the image" path.

**The setup script and the Flutter install are corpus-owned — this repo keeps no
copy of either** (PR #14, adopting Claudinite's pack-driven env model). The generic
setup script lives in the synced corpus at `.claudinite/environment-setup.sh`; it
syncs the corpus, sets git hygiene, and runs `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs
install`, and the **`flutter` pack** (declared in this repo) is what carries the
SDK install + pinned version. So there is **no** project-local
`.claude/environment-setup.sh` and **no** `.claude/hooks/check-environment.sh` /
`ENV_SETUP_VERSION` flag any more — don't recreate them; change the install by
editing the flutter pack upstream, not by adding a bespoke installer here.

**To set it up:** copy the full contents of the corpus
`.claudinite/environment-setup.sh` into the environment's *Setup script* field
(web UI → environment selector → edit environment → Setup script), then start a
fresh session so the snapshot rebuilds. (On a fresh clone that field is populated
after the first session's `sync-claudinite.sh` hook lands the corpus copy.)

**Validation.** The SessionStart hook `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs check` (wired
in `.claude/settings.json`) asserts Flutter is present each cloud session and, if
not, injects context telling Claude to alert the user to (re-)paste the corpus
setup script and restart. It is **ordered after `sync-claudinite.sh`** on purpose:
`env.mjs` lives under the synced `.claudinite/`, so the corpus must be populated
---
## Shared portable rules (the Claudinite canon)

The project-agnostic **canon** — general software-engineering practices, agentic
best practices, portable git/GitHub procedures, general working discipline,
unattended-agent architecture principles, and the repo owner's personal
interaction preferences — is **not** maintained in this repo. It lives in the
shared [`Claudinite`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite) repo and is
**mounted read-only** via Claudinite's **Method B**: the
[`.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh`](.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh) SessionStart
hook downloads the latest `main` as a codeload tarball over plain HTTPS at the
start of every session, so the canon stays current with no pin to bump. (Why
Method B and not a submodule: in a Claude Code web session the in-session git
remote is a git-only proxy scoped to this repo, so `git submodule update --init`
403s on the separate Claudinite repo and the clone-time gitlink drifts the working
tree — the HTTPS tarball sidesteps both. See
[issue #364](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/issues/364).)

The synced canon is **gitignored**; the tracked exceptions are the sync hook
itself (the one-glance signal that this repo mounts Claudinite, preserved by
the hook across each refresh) and this repo's own
[`.claudinite/local_packs/`](.claudinite/local_packs/) (project content, not
canon — likewise preserved across the sync's directory swap). A fresh checkout
that hasn't run the hook yet has nothing to import until the next session start
(or running the hook by hand) populates the mount;
`.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh` primes it once per environment. Pin to
a specific commit instead of tracking `main` by setting `CLAUDINITE_REF`.

The canon is consumed **read-only**; lessons are *captured locally* into the
local packs (above). Reconciling the local packs with the canon is Claudinite's
responsibility, not this repo's; the canon's own updates arrive here
automatically the next time the sync hook pulls `main`.
---

dev/requirements/shared/.artifacts/

# Stray clone left by an earlier submodule mount that some environments checked
# out at the wrong path; ignore it.
docs/

# Claudinite shared-rules canon: synced in by the SessionStart hook
# (.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh) as a read-only artifact, never committed
# -- so ignore the synced contents EXCEPT the committed hook at

---

# Generated/derived files — regenerated by `npm run regen`, never hand-merged.
# On a merge conflict the `ours` driver keeps one side automatically; you then
# rerun the generator and commit the result, so there's nothing to resolve by
# hand (and the PNGs are binary — they can't be 3-way merged at all). The driver
# is per-clone git config set by .claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh (and the
# one-time local setup in .claudinite/local_packs/gcec/RULES.md); a clone without
# it just falls back to a normal conflicting merge — still resolvable, only
# manual. A stale artifact can't slip through: its own test fails (the load-order
# drift guard, the UI snapshot pixel diff, the coverage gate). See the
---
  **not** on the `ours` driver; reconcile via `npm run regen` + the gallery
  drift gate. If `regen` reports a coverage regression, that's the real gate
  firing — review it, don't paper over it.
- **One-time local git setup** (the cloud sandbox gets it from
  `.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh`): `git config rerere.enabled true`
  and `git config merge.ours.driver true`. Without them nothing breaks — the
  generated files just fall back to a manual conflicting merge.
- **Keep divergence small**: when starting work on a branch, `git merge
  origin/main` (or rebase) **and run `npm run regen`** first, so the branch
  carries freshly-generated artifacts, not just the latest sources.
- **Whenever a change regenerates the UI gallery** (via `npm run refresh:ui`),
---
  per-source choice.
- **A supported/registered host is necessary but not sufficient for "an
  event"** — gating on `Boolean(site)` surfaced phantom events on home pages
  (#133); a real event requires actual data (JSON-LD or a parsed date), never a
  mere host match.
- **The mounted `.claudinite` canon can be stale mid-session** — re-run
  `.claudinite/mount/sync-claudinite.sh` by hand and re-check **before**
  concluding an upstream fix hasn't synced and **before committing a workaround
  for a check finding** (an accept, a suppression pragma): a stale mount twice
  produced spurious findings whose fixes had already merged (#664, #665).
- **The cloud Setup script runs as root starting in the repo's parent dir**,
  not the checkout — a bare `npm ci` there silently installs nothing;
  `.claudinite/mount/environment-setup.sh` `cd`s into the checkout first
  (#186/#196).

# TLDR — working instructions

The single entry-point for getting productive in this repo: set it up, change it, verify a change, and
ship it, without reverse-engineering the tree first. This is the **project-specific** layer — the
general working discipline (problem-first, the issue→branch→PR lifecycle, warnings-are-errors) is
auto-injected from the Claudinite corpus at session start (see [`.claudinite/CLAUDE.md`](../../.claudinite/CLAUDE.md));
this doc restates none of it and instead links where a rule already lives.

Routing index, not a payload: read the linked doc when its trigger fires. For the lessons that layer on
top of the technology packs, [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) in this folder is the router.

## What TLDR is

Community tl;dr notes for any web page: a signed-in user reads and posts short notes ("comments")
attached to a page, keyed by its normalized URL. It is a **monorepo** composing two deployable
artifacts joined by one shared rule:

- **`extension/`** — an MV3 Chrome extension (side panel, no bundler; Chrome loads the ES modules directly). UI-facing. Its unit tests live in the sibling `extension-test/`.
- **`server/`** — an AWS serverless backend (SAM: HTTP API v2 + Google-JWT authorizer, one Lambda, DynamoDB). One CloudFormation stack (app).
- **`shared/`** — the single source of truth for URL→`pageId` normalization and category constants, vendored byte-identically into both sides and drift-guarded.

Trust boundary: **public reads; authenticated writes** (Google ID token → JWT authorizer, POST only). The as-built design and every decision's rationale live in [`dev/docs/architecture.md`](../docs/architecture.md); the top-level map is the [README](../../README.md).

## Set up from a clean clone

- **Runtime:** Node ≥ 22 (`node --test` / `node --check` are the whole toolchain — no bundler, no linter). Deploying the backend also needs the AWS SAM CLI + AWS credentials; developing the extension needs only Chrome.
- **Per-package installs are independent** — each sub-package has its own `package.json`/lockfile; there is no root `node_modules`. The root, `extension/`, and `shared/` need **no** dependencies at all; only `server/` and `dev/` do.

```bash
npm test                                       # root: URL-normalizer corpus + shared drift guard (no deps)
npm --prefix server ci && npm --prefix server test   # handler logic, DynamoDB mocked at the SDK boundary
npm --prefix extension test                    # extension unit (in extension-test/) + manifest/packaging guards (no deps)
npm --prefix dev ci && npm --prefix dev test         # the executable-requirements suite (extension UI + server)
```

`npm run test:all` (from the root) chains all four in order — that is the single command that must be green (see [Verify a change](#verify-a-change)).

> **The `.claudinite/` mount.** The Claudinite corpus is gitignored and hydrated at session start by
> `.claude/hooks/sync-claudinite.sh` (it downloads `missingbulb/Claudinite`). Never commit `.claudinite/`;
> if it's missing, the SessionStart hook (or `CLAUDINITE_REF=<ref>` to pin) repopulates it.

## Run it / see it work

- **Extension, unpacked:** `chrome://extensions` → enable Developer mode → **Load unpacked** → select `extension/`. A plain checkout is **dev-pointed** (the committed `extension/config.mjs` `API_BASE_URL` targets the dev stack on purpose — a non-release build never talks to prod). Open the side panel from the toolbar; it fetches comments only while open, for the active tab, on commentable pages. Details + the dev/prod injection model: [`dev/docs/extension.md`](../docs/extension.md).
- **Server:** iterate against the **dev** stack (`tldr-app-dev`, its own DynamoDB table — a dev write can never reach prod). Deploy/seed commands and the dev-vs-prod promotion model: [`server/README.md`](../../server/README.md); the separate dev AWS account is the [dev-account runbook](../docs/dev-account-runbook.md).
- **See a *change* actually work — executable requirements.** A product requirement is stated once in [`dev/requirements/requirements.md`](../requirements/requirements.md) and proven by a case (a rendered-panel snapshot, a click-behavior test, or a pure rule). This is how a UI or server-contract change is *observed*, not just compiled. After an **intentional** panel change, regenerate the goldens: `npm --prefix dev run refresh:ui`. Method + invariants: [`dev/requirements/README.md`](../requirements/README.md) and the portable [ui-testing-guideline](../docs/ui-testing-guideline.md).

## Verify a change

- **Green means `npm run test:all` passes** — the same jobs CI runs (the `test: extension` and `test: requirements` workflows, plus the root drift guard). Reading CI to gate a merge: use the PR-scoped check state, not the run-listing tools — see [`ci-cd.md`](ci-cd.md).
- **The test bar:** every requirement is backed by a proof, and the suite goes red the moment a leaf is added to `requirements.md` without a case claiming it. Match the existing kind for the change (snapshot / behavior / logic). General test discipline (see-it-fail, snapshot rules) is the corpus `writing-tests` skill.
- **The expected is owner-owned — never edit an expected (or weaken an assertion) to turn a red requirement green.** On a mismatch, surface *actual vs expected* and ask. This is the executable-requirements contract, not a style preference.
- **Shared code is a single source of truth.** After editing anything under `shared/`, run `npm run sync-shared` to regenerate the vendored `*.GENERATED.mjs` copies in `server/src/vendor/` and `extension/vendor/`, and commit them; the root drift guard (`dev/build/tools/test/shared-drift.test.mjs`) fails on any byte divergence.

## Make & propose a change

- **Lifecycle (from the corpus, enforced by the `task-lifecycle` check):** open a GitHub issue → develop on a branch → reference the issue in commits (`Refs/Fixes/Closes #N`) → open a PR. The advanced git/GitHub procedures (commit layering, CI-trigger rules, recovering after a squash-merge) are the `git-github-advanced` skill.
- **Definition of done:** `npm run test:all` is green; any `shared/` edit is followed by `sync-shared` + committed generated copies; an intentional UI change has refreshed goldens and a matching requirement/case; a public API change follows the additive-only versioning policy ([architecture](../docs/architecture.md) §9.1); nothing owner-specific or generated-but-uncommitted is left dangling (see [Continuity](#continuity--handoff)).
- **Releasing is version-driven and mostly automatic** — the user-visible version is `extension/manifest.json`'s `version`; merging a bump to `main` cuts the GitHub Release and the daily auto-release ships shipped-file changes to the Chrome Web Store. Touch the release workflows **only** when cutting/publishing a release — this repo's instance of the shared chrome-extension-release standard (the canon guide in Claudinite owns the cross-repo contract and manual store procedures).

## Match the conventions

- **Native tooling only:** ES modules everywhere (`"type": "module"`), `node --test` for logic and `node --check` for the `chrome.*` glue that can't run headless. No bundler, no linter, no transpile step — a change that adds one is a departure, not an improvement.
- **Least privilege by default:** the manifest carries no `<all_urls>`/`host_permissions` (the API is reached via the server's `*` CORS); the deploy role is scoped to this stack's resources, not `AdministratorAccess`. Preserve both.
- **Before touching a subsystem, read its lesson** (routed from [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md)): [`chrome-extension.md`](chrome-extension.md) for `extension/`, [`aws-sam.md`](aws-sam.md) for `server/` + the build, [`ci-cd.md`](ci-cd.md) for `.github/workflows/`. Notably: a boundary faked in more than one double (the extension's `chrome.*` has two) must be taught in **every** double, or a missed call fails far from the change as a snapshot pixel-diff.

## Continuity / handoff

What a fresh session needs to resume — and what must and must not be committed:

- **Commit:** source, tests, requirement cases + their owner-approved goldens, and the regenerated `shared/*` → `*.GENERATED.mjs` vendored copies (they are checked in and drift-guarded, not build output).
- **Never commit:** `.claudinite/` (hydrated per session), `extension/config.local.json` (owner-only), `node_modules/`, `dist/`/`.aws-sam/`/`*.zip` build output, and the `dev/requirements/shared/.artifacts/` mismatch debris (the committed goldens are the truth). The `.gitignore` already lists these.
- **Config is injected, never committed:** prod URLs, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, and the extension `key` live as GitHub repository **variables** and are injected into the zip only by the release workflow; committed source stays placeholder/dev-pointed. See [`dev/docs/extension.md`](../docs/extension.md).
- **Open product/config questions** the owner still owns are tracked in [architecture](../docs/architecture.md) §11.

## Project-specific procedures

@dev/procedures/CLAUDE.md

content.subject: Simplify the required consumer .gitignore
content.description: Reduce the consumer .gitignore contract to the minimum (hook-log lines only), updating bootstrap.md, the flip note, mount/DESIGN.md, and EdFringeNow's .gitignore; document the transition trade-off (a stale environment's stray sync becomes visible untracked noise instead of hidden).

content.subject: Resolve the CI conformance stub question (item 2)
content.description: User believes claudinite-checks-ci.yml is a nightly action and wants it gone. It's actually the per-PR/push conformance backstop the design added deliberately. Surface the fact, ask remove-vs-keep, then apply to canon (baseline stub + flip note) and EdFringeNow.

content.subject: Minimize the root CLAUDE.md corpus index
content.description: The vendored CLAUDE.md reads like a README explaining Claudinite's architecture. Strip it to the few lines Claude actually needs in-context; relocate the explanatory map to README.md/docs for maintainers. Check no check/doc depends on the removed text.

content.subject: Consolidate the engine under engine/ (hooks, loaders, check_the_world/work, helpers, mount)
content.description: Relocate packs/registry.mjs, load-active-prose.mjs, env.mjs, interview.mjs to core/pack_loader/; update all imports, MACHINERY_ROOTS/ENGINE_DIR_ROOTS, session-start.sh, mount-skills dynamic import, docs, tests; run repo-text-sweeps discipline.

content.subject: Re-vendor EdFringeNow from the changed canon and push
content.description: After canon changes land on the branch: re-run apply-vendor against EdFringeNow, apply the simplified .gitignore, CI-stub decision, clean legacy leftovers in the container, run the sweep, commit and push (rides PR #73). Also answer user's item 4 (convergence deletes removed files — yes) in the report.

content.subject: Move skills into their owning packs, drop pack.skills key + skill-ownership check
content.description: git mv each skills/<s> into packs/<owner>/skills/<s> (canon) or .claudinite/local_packs/canon-curation/skills/ (the two curation skills); bundled skills = the dir listing so the pack.mjs skills key goes away; mount-skills walks active packs' skills dirs; vendor.mjs drops the union+extraSkills; delete skill-ownership; update catalog + catalog-completeness + all references.

content.subject: Drop empty rules/accept boilerplate from declarations
content.description: --init seeds only { packs, maintenance }; EdFringeNow's .claudinite-checks.json loses its empty "rules"/"accept" keys; confirm loadConfig defaults cover absence.

content.subject: Rename delivery "auto" → "auto-merge"; stop-command router; phase-3 retirement ledger; re-vendor EdFringeNow
content.description: Stop wiring routes via stable engine/hooks/stop-command.mjs (done in tree); maintenance.delivery canonical value becomes auto-merge (aliases auto/push/pr); consolidate the full phase-3 retirement checklist into engine/mount/DESIGN.md; commit+push canon; re-vendor EdFringeNow with new settings value and Stop command; push.

content.subject: Merge both PRs per "lgtm on both"
content.description: Gate Claudinite #384 on CI (nullglob fix pushed as 3a87a20), squash-merge #384 then EdFringeNow #73, sync local mains, run capture-log (--issue 385 canon, --issue 72 EdFringeNow), close issues #385 and #72 as completed.

content.subject: Finalize phase 2 (vendored mount) across the whole fleet
content.description: Add all other missingbulb repos, determine each repo's mount state (pre-flip / half-flipped / flipped / non-member), run the vendored-mount conversion per the flip note on every covered member, widen canon flip.repos to 'fleet', open the Setup-script re-paste issue per converted member.

200	**The setup script and the Flutter install are corpus-owned — this repo keeps no
201	copy of either** (PR #14, adopting Claudinite's pack-driven env model). The generic
202	setup script lives in the synced corpus at `.claudinite/environment-setup.sh`; it
203	syncs the corpus, sets git hygiene, and runs `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs
204	install`, and the **`flutter` pack** (declared in this repo) is what carries the
205	SDK install + pinned version. So there is **no** project-local
206	`.claude/environment-setup.sh` and **no** `.claude/hooks/check-environment.sh` /
207	`ENV_SETUP_VERSION` flag any more — don't recreate them; change the install by
208	editing the flutter pack upstream, not by adding a bespoke installer here.
209	
210	**To set it up:** copy the full contents of the corpus
211	`.claudinite/environment-setup.sh` into the environment's *Setup script* field
212	(web UI → environment selector → edit environment → Setup script), then start a
213	fresh session so the snapshot rebuilds. (On a fresh clone that field is populated
214	after the first session's `sync-claudinite.sh` hook lands the corpus copy.)
215	
216	**Validation.** The SessionStart hook `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs check` (wired
217	in `.claude/settings.json`) asserts Flutter is present each cloud session and, if
218	not, injects context telling Claude to alert the user to (re-)paste the corpus
219	setup script and restart. It is **ordered after `sync-claudinite.sh`** on purpose:
220	`env.mjs` lives under the synced `.claudinite/`, so the corpus must be populated
221	before it can run.

file.content:
step is skipped). This is the "download once, cache in the image" path.

**The setup script and the Flutter install are corpus-owned — this repo keeps no
copy of either** (PR #14, adopting Claudinite's pack-driven env model). The generic
setup script lives in the synced corpus at `.claudinite/environment-setup.sh`; it
syncs the corpus, sets git hygiene, and runs `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs
install`, and the **`flutter` pack** (declared in this repo) is what carries the
SDK install + pinned version. So there is **no** project-local
`.claude/environment-setup.sh` and **no** `.claude/hooks/check-environment.sh` /
`ENV_SETUP_VERSION` flag any more — don't recreate them; change the install by
editing the flutter pack upstream, not by adding a bespoke installer here.

**To set it up:** copy the full contents of the corpus
`.claudinite/environment-setup.sh` into the environment's *Setup script* field
(web UI → environment selector → edit environment → Setup script), then start a
fresh session so the snapshot rebuilds. (On a fresh clone that field is populated
after the first session's `sync-claudinite.sh` hook lands the corpus copy.)

**Validation.** The SessionStart hook `node .claudinite/packs/env.mjs check` (wired
in `.claude/settings.json`) asserts Flutter is present each cloud session and, if
not, injects context telling Claude to alert the user to (re-)paste the corpus
setup script and restart. It is **ordered after `sync-claudinite.sh`** on purpose:
`env.mjs` lives under the synced `.claudinite/`, so the corpus must be populated
before it can run.

input.new_string:
**The setup script and the Flutter install are corpus-owned — this repo keeps no
copy of either** (PR #14, adopting Claudinite's pack-driven env model). The generic
setup script is the vendored corpus's
`.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh`; it sets git
hygiene and runs `node .claudinite/shared/engine/pack_loader/env-requirements.mjs
install`, and the **`flutter` pack** (declared in this repo) is what carries the
SDK install + pinned version. So there is **no** project-local
`.claude/environment-setup.sh` and **no** `.claude/hooks/check-environment.sh` /
`ENV_SETUP_VERSION` flag any more — don't recreate them; change the install by
editing the flutter pack upstream, not by adding a bespoke installer here.

**To set it up:** copy the full contents of
`.claudinite/shared/engine/hooks/environment-setup-command.sh` into the
environment's *Setup script* field (web UI → environment selector → edit
environment → Setup script), then start a fresh session so the snapshot rebuilds.

**Validation.** The session-start hook runs
`node .claudinite/shared/engine/pack_loader/env-requirements.mjs check`, which
asserts Flutter is present each cloud session and, if not, injects context
telling Claude to alert the user to (re-)paste the corpus setup script and
restart. The corpus is tracked (vendored), so the check can always run — no
sync ordering to worry about.
